ASPECT-Landlab Test Python Framework
  • Setup Installation
  • View page source
Previous Next

Setup Installation

This guide summarizes the minimal steps required to install, configure, and run a coupled ASPECT–Landlab simulation using Python (mpi4py).

1. Python Environment (uv)

Install and manage Python using uv:

brew install uv
mkdir /Users/biraj/software/landlab_aspect
cd /Users/biraj/software/landlab_aspect
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/landlab-aspect/aspect
cd aspect
uv venv --python 3.12
source .venv/bin/activate
uv sync
uv pip install numpy meshio (optional)

2. Install ASPECT with Python Support

Compile ASPECT with deal.II and enable Python:

cd /Users/biraj/software/landlab_aspect/aspect
mkdir build
cd build
cmake \
  -DDEAL_II_DIR=/Users/biraj/software/dealii/dealii-9.7.1/install/deal.II-v9.7.0 \
  -DASPECT_WITH_PYTHON=ON \
  -DPython3_EXECUTABLE=$(which python) \
  ..
 make -j8
 #Done

Critical flags:

  • ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON=ON → enables coupling

  • Python3_EXECUTABLE → must point to uv/conda environment

3. Coupling Mechanism (C++ ↔ Python)

ASPECT uses the Landlab mesh deformation plugin:

  • Loads Python module

  • Calls required functions:

    • initialize()

    • set_mesh_information()

    • get_grid_x(), get_grid_y()

    • update_until()

    • get_initial_topography()

Core bridge logic (C++):

  • Python module imported dynamically

  • MPI communicator passed to Python

  • Surface nodes exchanged each timestep

  • Python returns elevation change → converted to velocity

▶ Click to expand landlab.cc 
  1/*
  2  Copyright (C) 2025 - 2025 by the authors of the ASPECT code.
  3
  4  This file is part of ASPECT.

  7  it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
  8  the Free Software Foundation; either version 2, or (at your option)
  9  any later version.
 10
 11  ASPECT is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
 12  but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
 13  MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
 14  GNU General Public License for more details.
 15
 16  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
 17  along with ASPECT; see the file LICENSE.  If not see
 18  <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 19 */
 20
 21
 22#include <aspect/mesh_deformation/landlab.h>
 23#include <deal.II/base/exceptions.h>
 24#include <deal.II/base/patterns.h>
 25#if DEAL_II_VERSION_GTE(9,8,0)
 26#include <deal.II/numerics/data_out_points.h>
 27#endif
 28
 29#include <aspect/gravity_model/interface.h>
 30#include <aspect/geometry_model/chunk.h>
 31#include <aspect/geometry_model/sphere.h>
 32#include <aspect/geometry_model/spherical_shell.h>
 33#include <deal.II/base/array_view.h>
 34
 35#include <cfenv>
 36
 37#include <aspect/python_helper.h>
 38
 39#ifdef ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON
 40
 41using namespace dealii;
 42
 43/**
 44 * Call a Python function from module @p pModule with name @p func_name
 45 * Returns the result (caller must Py_DECREF). Throws on error.
 46 */
 47PyObject *call_python_function(PyObject *pModule, const char *func_name, PyObject *pArgs = nullptr)
 48{
 49  PyObject *pFunc = PyObject_GetAttrString(pModule, func_name);
 50  if (!pFunc || !PyCallable_Check(pFunc))
 51    AssertThrow(false, ExcMessage(std::string("Failed to load function: ") + func_name));
 52
 53  PyObject *pValue = PyObject_CallObject(pFunc, pArgs);
 54  Py_DECREF(pFunc);
 55
 56  if (pValue == nullptr)
 57    {
 58      if (PyErr_Occurred())
 59        PyErr_Print();
 60      AssertThrow(false, ExcMessage(std::string(func_name) + " returned NULL"));
 61    }
 62  return pValue;
 63}
 64
 65#endif
 66
 67
 68
 69
 70namespace aspect
 71{
 72
 73  namespace MeshDeformation
 74  {
 75    template <int dim>
 76    Landlab<dim>::Landlab()
 77    {
 78    }
 79
 80
 81
 82    template <int dim>
 83    void
 84    Landlab<dim>::initialize ()
 85    {
 86      // Determine whether we are in a spherical geometry or not, which affects how we interpret the coordinates of the evaluation points.
 87      if (Plugins::plugin_type_matches<GeometryModel::Chunk<dim>>(this->get_geometry_model()) ||
 88          Plugins::plugin_type_matches<GeometryModel::SphericalShell<dim>>(this->get_geometry_model()) ||
 89          Plugins::plugin_type_matches<GeometryModel::Sphere<dim>>(this->get_geometry_model()))
 90        is_spherical = true;
 91      else
 92        is_spherical = false;
 93
 94      unsigned int rank = Utilities::MPI::this_mpi_process(this->get_mpi_communicator());
 95
 96      this_rank_runs_landlab = (rank < n_landlab_ranks);
 97      const int color = this_rank_runs_landlab?1:0;
 98      const int ierr = MPI_Comm_split(this->get_mpi_communicator(), color, rank, &landlab_communicator);
 99      AssertThrow(ierr == MPI_SUCCESS, ExcMessage("Failed to split MPI communicator for Landlab simulation"));
100
101      if (this_rank_runs_landlab)
102        {
103#ifdef ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON
104          // Append script dirs so env packages (venv site-packages, PYTHONPATH) are found first
105          // for "import landlab":
106          PyRun_SimpleString("import sys");
107          PyRun_SimpleString("sys.path.append(\"" ASPECT_SOURCE_DIR "/tests\")");
108          PyRun_SimpleString("sys.path.append(\".\")");
109
110          // avoid floating point exceptions in Landlab Python code:
111#ifdef ASPECT_USE_FP_EXCEPTIONS
112          fedisableexcept(FE_DIVBYZERO|FE_INVALID);
113#endif
114
115          std::cout << "importing '" << script_module_name << "' ..." << std::endl;
116          pModule = PyImport_ImportModule(script_module_name.c_str());
117          if (PyErr_Occurred())
118            PyErr_Print();
119          AssertThrow(pModule, ExcMessage("Failed to load Python module"));
120
121          // Call Python initialize() function with communicator handle
122          PyObject *pArgs;
123          if (n_landlab_ranks == 1)
124            pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(1, Py_None);
125          else
126            pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(1, PyLong_FromLong(MPI_Comm_c2f(landlab_communicator)));
127          PyObject *pValue = call_python_function(pModule, "initialize", pArgs);
128
129          Py_DECREF(pArgs);
130          Py_DECREF(pValue);
131
132#else
133          AssertThrow(false, ExcMessage("ASPECT needs to be configure with Python support "
134                                        "(ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON=ON in CMake) to be able to use the Landlab mesh deformation model."));
135#endif
136        }
137    }
138
139
140
141    template <int dim>
142    void
143    Landlab<dim>::update ()
144    {
145
146#ifdef ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON
147      if (!this->remote_point_evaluator)
148        {
149          if (!this_rank_runs_landlab)
150            {
151              // This rank does not participate, so we don't own any evaluation points:
152              std::vector<Point<dim>> surface_points;
153              this->set_evaluation_points(surface_points);
154              return;
155            }
156
157          {
158            // set_mesh_information: call with None
159            PyObject *pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(1, Py_None);
160            PyObject *pValue = call_python_function(pModule, "set_mesh_information", pArgs);
161            Py_DECREF(pArgs);
162            Py_DECREF(pValue);
163          }
164
165          {
166            // get grid:
167            PyObject *pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(1, PyLong_FromLong(-1L));
168            PyObject *pgrid_x = call_python_function(pModule, "get_grid_x", pArgs);
169
170            PyObject *pgrid_y = nullptr;
171            PyObject *pgrid_z = nullptr;
172            if (dim == 3)
173              pgrid_y = call_python_function(pModule, "get_grid_y", pArgs);
174            if (dim == 3 && is_spherical)
175              pgrid_z = call_python_function(pModule, "get_grid_z", pArgs);
176            Py_DECREF(pArgs);
177
178            const ArrayView<double> data_x = PythonHelper::numpy_to_array_view(pgrid_x);
179            const ArrayView<double> data_y = (dim == 3)
180                                             ? PythonHelper::numpy_to_array_view(pgrid_y)
181                                             : ArrayView<double>(nullptr, 0);
182            // For 3D spherical models, we need the z coordinates of the LandLab grid to be able to
183            // correctly set the evaluation points.
184            const ArrayView<double> data_z = (dim == 3 && is_spherical)
185                                             ? PythonHelper::numpy_to_array_view(pgrid_z)
186                                             : ArrayView<double>(nullptr, 0);
187
188            if (dim == 3)
189              AssertThrow(data_x.size() == data_y.size(), ExcMessage("get_grid_x and get_grid_y returned different sizes"));
190            if (dim == 3 && is_spherical)
191              AssertThrow(data_x.size() == data_z.size(), ExcMessage("get_grid_x and get_grid_z returned different sizes"));
192
193            std::vector<Point<dim>> surface_points(data_x.size());
194            for (size_t i = 0; i < data_x.size(); i++)
195              {
196                const double depth = this->get_geometry_model().representative_point(0.0)[dim-1];
197                if (dim == 2)
198                  surface_points[i] = Point<dim>(data_x[i], depth);
199                else if (dim == 3)
200                  {
201                    const double z = (is_spherical) ? data_z[i] : depth;
202                    surface_points[i] = Point<dim>(data_x[i], data_y[i], z);
203                  }
204              }
205
206            // Clean up Python objects
207            Py_DECREF(pgrid_x);
208            if (pgrid_y)
209              Py_DECREF(pgrid_y);
210            if (pgrid_z)
211              Py_DECREF(pgrid_z);
212
213            this->set_evaluation_points(surface_points);
214          }
215        }
216#endif
217    }
218
219
220
221    template <int dim>
222    std::vector<Tensor<1,dim>>
223    Landlab<dim>::compute_updated_velocities_at_points (const std::vector<std::vector<double>> &current_solution_at_points) const
224    {
225#ifdef ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON
226      Assert(current_solution_at_points.size() == this->evaluation_points.size(), ExcInternalError());
227      std::vector<Tensor<1,dim>> velocities(current_solution_at_points.size(), Tensor<1,dim>());
228
229      if (this_rank_runs_landlab)
230        {
231          // Build a dictionary with solution values for each variable to pass to Python:
232          PyObject *pDict = PyDict_New();
233          std::vector<std::string> variable_names = { "x velocity", "y velocity"};
234          if (dim == 3)
235            variable_names.push_back("z velocity");
236
237          variable_names.push_back("pressure");
238          variable_names.push_back("temperature");
239
240          // Add compositional fields to the variable names and variable data:
241          for (unsigned int c=0; c<this->n_compositional_fields(); ++c)
242            variable_names.push_back(this->introspection().name_for_compositional_index(c));
243
244          std::vector<std::vector<double>> variable_data(variable_names.size(),  std::vector<double>(current_solution_at_points.size(), 0.0));
245          for (unsigned int i=0; i<variable_names.size(); ++i)
246            {
247              for (unsigned int j=0; j<current_solution_at_points.size(); ++j)
248                {
249                  variable_data[i][j] = current_solution_at_points[j][i];
250                }
251            }
252          for (unsigned int i=0; i<variable_names.size(); ++i)
253            {
254              auto pValue = PythonHelper::vector_to_numpy_object(variable_data[i]);
255              PyDict_SetItemString(pDict, variable_names[i].c_str(), pValue.get());
256            }
257
258          // Call update_until()
259          PyObject *pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(2, PyFloat_FromDouble(this->get_time()), pDict);
260          PyObject *pValue = call_python_function(pModule, "update_until", pArgs);
261          Py_DECREF(pDict);
262          Py_DECREF(pArgs);
263
264          const ArrayView<const double> data = PythonHelper::numpy_to_array_view(pValue);
265          const double one_over_dt = 1.0 / ((this->get_timestep() > 0.0) ? this->get_timestep() : 1.0);
266          for (size_t i=0; i<data.size(); ++i)
267            {
268              const Tensor<1,dim> gravity = this->get_gravity_model().gravity_vector(this->evaluation_points[i]);
269
270              Tensor<1,dim> topography_direction;
271              if (gravity.norm() > 0.0)
272                topography_direction = -gravity / gravity.norm();
273
274              velocities[i] = topography_direction * data[i] * one_over_dt;
275            }
276
277          Py_DECREF(pValue);
278        }
279
280      // Produce debug output as a vtu file
281#if DEAL_II_VERSION_GTE(9,8,0)
282      {
283        static unsigned int output_no = 0;
284
285        DataOutPoints<dim, dim> out;
286        //const auto &mapping = this->get_mapping();
287        std::vector<Point<dim>> real_evaluation_points(this->evaluation_points.size());
288        std::vector<std::vector<double>> data(this->evaluation_points.size(), std::vector<double>(dim, 0.0));
289        const double one_over_dt = 1.0 / ((this->get_timestep() > 0.0) ? this->get_timestep() : 1.0);
290
291        for (unsigned int i=0; i<this->evaluation_points.size(); ++i)
292          {
293            real_evaluation_points[i] = this->evaluation_points[i];  // TODO: use mapping to compute real position
294            for (unsigned int c=0; c<dim; ++c)
295              data[i][c] = velocities[i][c] * one_over_dt;
296          }
297
298        const std::vector<std::string> data_component_names(dim, "velocity");
299        const std::vector<DataComponentInterpretation::DataComponentInterpretation> data_component_interpretations(dim, DataComponentInterpretation::component_is_part_of_vector);
300
301        out.build_patches(real_evaluation_points, 0, data, data_component_names, data_component_interpretations);
302
303        out.write_vtu_with_pvtu_record(this->get_output_directory(), "surf_points", output_no, this->get_mpi_communicator(), 4, 0);
304
305        ++output_no;
306      }
307#endif
308
309      return velocities;
310#else
311      (void)current_solution_at_points;
312      return std::vector<Tensor<1,dim>>();
313#endif
314    }
315
316
317
318    template <int dim>
319    void Landlab<dim>::
320    compute_initial_deformation_as_constraints(const Mapping<dim> &/*mapping*/,
321                                               const DoFHandler<dim> &mesh_deformation_dof_handler,
322                                               const types::boundary_id boundary_indicator,
323                                               AffineConstraints<double> &constraints) const
324    {
325#ifdef ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON
326      // hack: cast away const and call update() so we get the evaluation point
327      // datastructures populated:
328      const_cast<Landlab<dim>*>(this)->update();
329
330      // 1. Grab initial deformation from Landlab:
331      std::vector<Tensor<1,dim>> initial_deformation(this->evaluation_points.size(), Tensor<1,dim>());
332      if (this_rank_runs_landlab)
333        {
334          PyObject *pDict = PyDict_New();
335          PyDict_SetItemString(pDict, "ASPECT Dimension", PyLong_FromLong(dim));
336
337          PyObject *pArgs = PyTuple_Pack(1,
338                                         pDict
339                                        );
340          Py_DECREF(pDict);
341          PyObject *pValue = call_python_function(pModule, "get_initial_topography", pArgs);
342          Py_DECREF(pArgs);
343          ArrayView<double> data = PythonHelper::numpy_to_array_view(pValue);
344
345          for (size_t i=0; i<data.size(); ++i)
346            {
347              const Tensor<1,dim> gravity = this->get_gravity_model().gravity_vector(this->evaluation_points[i]);
348              Tensor<1,dim> topography_direction;
349              if (gravity.norm() > 0.0)
350                topography_direction = -gravity / gravity.norm();
351
352              initial_deformation[i] = data[i] * topography_direction;
353            }
354          Py_DECREF(pValue);
355        }
356
357      // 2. Interpolate deformation into a DoF vector:
358      LinearAlgebra::Vector initial_deformation_dof_vector = this->interpolate_external_velocities_to_surface_support_points(initial_deformation);
359      const DoFHandler<dim> &mesh_dof_handler = this->get_mesh_deformation_handler().get_mesh_deformation_dof_handler();
360      const IndexSet mesh_locally_relevant = DoFTools::extract_locally_relevant_dofs (mesh_dof_handler);
361      LinearAlgebra::Vector initial_deformation_ghosted(mesh_dof_handler.locally_owned_dofs(),
362                                                        mesh_locally_relevant,
363                                                        this->get_mpi_communicator());
364      initial_deformation_ghosted = initial_deformation_dof_vector;
365
366      const IndexSet constrained_dofs =
367        DoFTools::extract_boundary_dofs(mesh_deformation_dof_handler,
368                                        ComponentMask(dim, true),
369      {boundary_indicator});
370
371      // 3. Add constraints from DoF values:
372      for (const types::global_dof_index index : constrained_dofs)
373        {
374          if (constraints.can_store_line(index))
375            if (constraints.is_constrained(index)==false)
376              {
377#if DEAL_II_VERSION_GTE(9,6,0)
378                constraints.add_constraint(index,
379                                           {},
380                                           initial_deformation_ghosted(index));
381#else
382                constraints.add_line(index);
383                constraints.set_inhomogeneity(index, initial_deformation_ghosted(index));
384#endif
385              }
386        }
387#else
388      (void)mesh_deformation_dof_handler;
389      (void)boundary_indicator;
390      (void)constraints;
391#endif
392    }
393
394
395
396    template <int dim>
397    void Landlab<dim>::declare_parameters(ParameterHandler &prm)
398    {
399      prm.enter_subsection("Mesh deformation");
400      {
401        prm.enter_subsection("Landlab");
402        {
403          prm.declare_entry("MPI ranks for Landlab", "1",
404                            Patterns::Integer(1),
405                            "Number of ranks to use for the Landlab simulation. If set to 1, the Landlab simulation will run sequentially "
406                            "without MPI. If set to -1, the Landlab simulation will run on all ranks.");
407          prm.declare_entry("Script path", "",
408                            Patterns::Anything(),
409                            "Path to the Python script to execute. Relative paths and the placeholders "
410                            "ASPECT_SOURCE_DIR and ASPECT_BINARY_DIR are allowed.");
411          prm.declare_entry("Script name", "",
412                            Patterns::Anything(),
413                            "Name of the Python module to load (without .py extension).");
414          prm.declare_entry("Script argument", "",
415                            Patterns::Anything(),
416                            "An arbitrary string to be passed to the initialize() function in the "
417                            "Python script. Can be used to specify a configuration file or other option.");
418        }
419        prm.leave_subsection();
420      }
421      prm.leave_subsection();
422    }
423
424
425
426    template <int dim>
427    void Landlab<dim>::parse_parameters(ParameterHandler &prm)
428    {
429      prm.enter_subsection ("Mesh deformation");
430      {
431        prm.enter_subsection ("Landlab");
432        {
433          const int ranks = prm.get_integer("MPI ranks for Landlab");
434          if (ranks == -1)
435            n_landlab_ranks = Utilities::MPI::n_mpi_processes(this->get_mpi_communicator());
436          else
437            n_landlab_ranks = ranks;
438
439          script_path = prm.get("Script path");
440          script_module_name = prm.get("Script name");
441          script_argument = prm.get("Script argument");
442        }
443        prm.leave_subsection ();
444      }
445      prm.leave_subsection ();
446    }
447  }
448}
449
450
451// explicit instantiation of the functions we implement in this file
452namespace aspect
453{
454  namespace MeshDeformation
455  {
456    ASPECT_REGISTER_MESH_DEFORMATION_MODEL(Landlab,
457                                           "Landlab",
458                                           "A mesh deformation plugin that lets a Python script control the "
459                                           "deformation of the surface. It is meant for coupling with the landscape evolution "
460                                           "code Landlab, but any other script that provides the necessary functions can be used. "
461                                           "It is necessary to have Python and numpy with their C APIs installed and that "
462                                           "ASPECT_WITH_PYTHON is enabled when ASPECT is configured with CMake.")
463  }
464}

Reference implementation: :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

4. Python Script (Landlab Side)

Key structure:

from mpi4py import MPI
from landlab import RasterModelGrid
from landlab.components import LinearDiffuser

def initialize(comm_handle):
    pass

def set_mesh_information(_):
    model_grid = RasterModelGrid((5,5), xy_spacing=0.25)

    z = np.random.rand(model_grid.number_of_nodes)

    elevation = model_grid.add_field(
        "topographic__elevation",
        z,
        at="node"
    )

def update_until(end_time, _):
    linear_diffuser.run_one_step(dt)
    return dz

def get_grid_x(_): return model_grid.node_x
def get_grid_y(_): return model_grid.node_y
def get_initial_topography(_): return elevation

Key features:

  • Raster grid must match ASPECT surface

  • Returns elevation change dz (NOT absolute elevation)

  • Uses mpi4py for parallel compatibility

▶ Click to expand random_noise_surface_test_squar_grid.py 
  1#!/usr/bin/env python3
  2print("Hello from gaussian_test.py")
  3
  4from mpi4py import MPI
  5import numpy as np
  6import os
  7from landlab import RasterModelGrid
  8from landlab.components import LinearDiffuser
  9
 10# ============================================
 11# OUTPUT DIRECTORY
 12# ============================================
 13OUTPUT_DIR = os.path.join("output", "random_noise_surface_test_vtu_squar_grid", "landlab_vtu")
 14
 15def ensure_output_dir():
 16    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
 17
 18
 19# ============================================
 20# OPTIONAL VTU SUPPORT
 21# ============================================
 22VTU_AVAILABLE = False
 23try:
 24    import meshio
 25    VTU_AVAILABLE = True
 26except Exception:
 27    VTU_AVAILABLE = False
 28
 29
 30# ============================================
 31# GLOBAL STATE
 32# ============================================
 33current_time = 0.0
 34comm = None
 35model_grid = None
 36elevation = None
 37linear_diffuser = None
 38
 39
 40# ============================================
 41# MPI INIT
 42# ============================================
 43def initialize(comm_handle):
 44    global comm
 45    if comm_handle is not None:
 46        comm = MPI.Comm.f2py(comm_handle)
 47        print(f"[Python] Rank {comm.rank}/{comm.size}")
 48    else:
 49        print("[Python] Running sequentially")
 50
 51
 52def finalize():
 53    pass
 54
 55
 56# ============================================
 57# VTU WRITER
 58# ============================================
 59def write_vtu_pvd(x, y, z, step_id):
 60    import meshio
 61
 62    ensure_output_dir()
 63
 64    points = np.column_stack((x, y, z))
 65    cells = [("vertex", np.arange(len(points)).reshape(-1, 1))]
 66
 67    filename = f"landlab_{step_id:06d}.vtu"
 68    filepath = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)
 69
 70    mesh = meshio.Mesh(
 71        points=points,
 72        cells=cells,
 73        point_data={"elevation": z}
 74    )
 75
 76    mesh.write(filepath)
 77
 78    # ---- PVD ----
 79    pvd_file = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "landlab_collection.pvd")
 80
 81    try:
 82        with open(pvd_file, "r") as f:
 83            lines = f.readlines()
 84    except FileNotFoundError:
 85        lines = [
 86            '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n',
 87            '<VTKFile type="Collection" version="0.1">\n',
 88            '  <Collection>\n',
 89            '  </Collection>\n',
 90            '</VTKFile>\n'
 91        ]
 92
 93    entry = f'    <DataSet timestep="{step_id}" file="{filename}"/>\n'
 94    lines.insert(-2, entry)
 95
 96    with open(pvd_file, "w") as f:
 97        f.writelines(lines)
 98
 99    print(f"[Output] VTU: {filepath}")
100
101
102# ============================================
103# FALLBACK VTK
104# ============================================
105def write_simple_vtk(filename, x, y, z):
106    ensure_output_dir()
107    filepath = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)
108
109    with open(filepath, "w") as f:
110        f.write("# vtk DataFile Version 3.0\n")
111        f.write("Landlab output\n")
112        f.write("ASCII\n")
113        f.write("DATASET POLYDATA\n")
114        f.write(f"POINTS {len(x)} float\n")
115        for i in range(len(x)):
116            f.write(f"{x[i]} {y[i]} {z[i]}\n")
117
118
119# ============================================
120# GRID + INITIAL CONDITION
121# ============================================
122def set_mesh_information(dict_grid_information):
123    global model_grid, elevation, linear_diffuser
124
125    if model_grid is None:
126        print("* Creating Raster grid (ASPECT-compatible)")
127
128        model_grid = RasterModelGrid((5, 5), xy_spacing=0.25)
129
130        x = model_grid.node_x
131        y = model_grid.node_y
132
133        # print(" Initializing Gaussian hill")
134
135        # z = np.exp(-((x - 0.5)**2 + (y - 0.5)**2) / 0.01)
136
137        # print(" Initializing linear slope")
138
139        # z = 0.5 * x
140
141        # Quadratic slope
142        # z = 0.5 * x + 0.2 * x**2
143        # z = 1.0 * (x - 0.5)**2
144
145        # Flat surface
146        # elevation = model_grid.add_zeros(
147        #     "topographic__elevation", at="node"
148        # )
149        # Random noise surface
150        np.random.seed(42)  # for reproducibility
151        z = np.random.rand(len(x))   # values between 0 and 1
152        
153        elevation = model_grid.add_field(
154            "topographic__elevation", z, at="node"
155        )
156
157        linear_diffuser = LinearDiffuser(
158            model_grid,
159            linear_diffusivity=50
160        )
161
162        print(f"* Nodes: {model_grid.number_of_nodes}")
163
164        if comm is None or comm.rank == 0:
165            # REQUIRED FOR TEST
166            np.savetxt("initial_elevation.txt", elevation)
167
168            # also save in output folder
169            ensure_output_dir()
170            np.savetxt(
171                os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "initial_elevation.txt"),
172                elevation
173            )
174
175
176# ============================================
177# TIME STEPPING
178# ============================================
179def update_until(end_time, dict_variable_name_to_value_in_nodes):
180    global current_time, elevation, linear_diffuser, model_grid
181
182    dt_total = end_time - current_time
183    dz = np.zeros(model_grid.number_of_nodes)
184
185    if dt_total > 0:
186        n = 10
187        dt = dt_total / n
188
189        for _ in range(n):
190            before = elevation.copy()
191            linear_diffuser.run_one_step(dt)
192            dz += elevation - before
193
194    current_time = end_time
195
196    write_output()
197
198    return dz
199
200
201def write_output():
202    global model_grid, elevation, current_time
203
204    if model_grid is None:
205        return
206
207    if comm is None or comm.rank == 0:
208        x = model_grid.node_x
209        y = model_grid.node_y
210        z = elevation
211
212        ensure_output_dir()
213
214        step_id = int(round(current_time / 1e-4))
215
216        # REQUIRED FOR TEST
217        np.savetxt("output_elevation.txt", z)
218
219        # (optional but good) also store in output folder
220        np.savetxt(
221            os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "output_elevation.txt"),
222            z
223        )
224
225        # debug xyz
226        np.savetxt(
227            os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, f"xyz_{step_id:06d}.txt"),
228            np.column_stack((x, y, z))
229        )
230
231        # VTU
232        if VTU_AVAILABLE:
233            try:
234                write_vtu_pvd(x, y, z, step_id)
235            except Exception as e:
236                print("VTU failed → fallback:", e)
237                write_simple_vtk(f"fallback_{step_id:06d}.vtk", x, y, z)
238        else:
239            write_simple_vtk(f"fallback_{step_id:06d}.vtk", x, y, z)
240# ============================================
241# REQUIRED INTERFACE
242# ============================================
243def get_grid_x(grid_id):
244    return model_grid.node_x
245
246
247def get_grid_y(grid_id):
248    return model_grid.node_y
249
250
251def get_initial_topography(grid_id):
252    return elevation

5. ASPECT .prm Configuration (Essential Parts)

Mesh deformation using Landlab in ASPECT:

subsection Mesh deformation
  set Mesh deformation boundary indicators = top: Landlab

  subsection Landlab
    set MPI ranks for Landlab = 1
    set Script name = random_noise_surface_test_squar_grid
    set Script path = .
  end
end

Note

random_noise_surface_test_squar_grid is the name of python script mentioned in point 4 above

Model setup (Geometry and Mesh Refinement) in ASPECT:

 set Dimension = 3
 set End time = 0.01
 set Maximum time step = 0.0001

 subsection Geometry model
   set Model name = box
   subsection Box
     set X extent = 1
     set Y extent = 1
     set Z extent = 1
   end
 end

subsection Mesh refinement
  set Initial global refinement                = 2
  set Initial adaptive refinement              = 0
  set Time steps between mesh refinement       = 0
end
▶ Click to expand ASPECT input random_noise_surface_test_squar_grid.prm 
  1#
  2
  3set Dimension                              = 3
  4set Use years instead of seconds           = false
  5set End time                               = 0.01
  6set Maximum time step                      = 0.0001
  7set Nonlinear solver scheme = no Advection, no Stokes
  8set Pressure normalization                 = surface
  9set Surface pressure                       = 0
 10set Output directory                       = output/random_noise_surface_test_vtu_squar_grid
 11
 12subsection Geometry model
 13  set Model name = box
 14
 15  subsection Box
 16    set X extent = 1
 17    set Y extent = 1
 18    set Z extent = 1
 19  end
 20end
 21
 22# Temperature effects are ignored
 23subsection Initial temperature model
 24  set Model name = function
 25
 26  subsection Function
 27    set Function expression = 1-z
 28  end
 29end
 30
 31subsection Boundary temperature model
 32  set Fixed temperature boundary indicators = bottom, top
 33  set List of model names = initial temperature
 34end
 35
 36# Free slip on all boundaries
 37subsection Boundary velocity model
 38  set Tangential velocity boundary indicators = left, right, bottom, top
 39end
 40
 41subsection Mesh deformation
 42  set Mesh deformation boundary indicators = top: Landlab
 43  set Additional tangential mesh velocity boundary indicators = left, right
 44
 45  subsection Landlab
 46    set MPI ranks for Landlab = 1
 47    set Script name = random_noise_surface_test_squar_grid
 48    set Script argument = 
 49    set Script path = .
 50  end 
 51end
 52
 53# Vertical gravity
 54subsection Gravity model
 55  set Model name = vertical
 56
 57  subsection Vertical
 58    set Magnitude = 1
 59  end
 60end
 61
 62# One material with unity properties
 63subsection Material model
 64  set Model name = simple
 65
 66  subsection Simple model
 67    set Reference density             = 1
 68    set Reference specific heat       = 1
 69    set Reference temperature         = 0
 70    set Thermal conductivity          = 1
 71    set Thermal expansion coefficient = 1
 72    set Viscosity                     = 1
 73  end
 74end
 75
 76# We also have to specify that we want to use the Boussinesq
 77# approximation (assuming the density in the temperature
 78# equation to be constant, and incompressibility).
 79subsection Formulation
 80  set Formulation = Boussinesq approximation
 81end
 82
 83# We here use a globally refined mesh without
 84# adaptive mesh refinement.
 85subsection Mesh refinement
 86  set Initial global refinement                = 2
 87  set Initial adaptive refinement              = 0
 88  set Time steps between mesh refinement       = 0
 89end
 90
 91subsection Postprocess
 92  set List of postprocessors = visualization,  topography
 93
 94  subsection Visualization
 95    set Time between graphical output = 0
 96    set Output mesh velocity = true
 97    set Output mesh displacement = true
 98    set Output undeformed mesh = false
 99    set Interpolate output = false
100  end
101end

6. Key Execution Flow

  1. ASPECT starts simulation

  2. MPI communicator split

  3. Python Landlab initialized

  4. Grid points requested from Python

  5. ASPECT sends surface solution fields

  6. Python evolves topography (diffusion)

  7. Python returns dz

  8. ASPECT converts to mesh velocity

  9. Mesh deforms accordingly

7. Important Notes

  • dz controls deformation → not absolute elevation

  • Ensure grid consistency (ASPECT ↔ Landlab)

  • MPI ranks can be split between ASPECT and Landlab

  • Output debugging via VTU/VTK recommended

8. Minimal Run Command

mpirun -np 4 ./aspect your_model.prm

Summary

Core requirements for coupling:

  • ASPECT compiled with Python support

  • Landlab Python script implementing required interface

  • MPI communication via mpi4py

  • Proper .prm configuration linking to Python script

This setup enables dynamic surface evolution in ASPECT driven by Landlab processes (e.g., diffusion, erosion, uplift).

Previous Next
